claim.content: Assume standard Fermi-liquid relations linking the low-energy derivative of the real self-energy to the quasiparticle residue and effective mass as follows: define $\lambda\equiv -\left.\frac{d\Sigma'(\varepsilon)}{d\varepsilon}\right|_{\varepsilon=0}$, define the quasiparticle residue $Z\equiv 1/(1+\lambda)$, define the band mass $m_{b}$ as the curvature-derived effective mass of the non-interacting DFT band at the Fermi momentum, and assume that the interacting quasiparticle effective mass satisfies $m^{*}/m_{b}=Z^{-1}$, with the additional assumptions that the electronic self-energy is approximately momentum-independent near the Fermi surface (local approximation) and that Fermi-liquid quasiparticle formulas apply in the low-energy regime considered.
